label: Localized cleaning product irritant rash without burn or anaphylaxis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized cleaning product irritant skin reaction.
No chemical burn, anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, eye involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Chemical burn, eye exposure, anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy/toxicology plan.
must_not_miss: Cellulitis or severe cutaneous adverse reaction.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, poison control as directed, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, chemical, ocular, infectious, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized cleaning product irritant skin reaction with chemical burn, anaphylaxis, infection, eye, mucosal, pregnancy/pediatric, severe-rash, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized cleaning product irritant rash
discharge diagnosis localized cleaning product irritant rash
localized cleaning product irritant rash diagnosed
